controls.title: Organizational Context
controls.description: The circumstances surrounding the organization's cybersecurity risk management decisions are understood, including the organization's mission, stakeholder expectations, legal and regulatory requirements, and the dependencies and criticality of the assets and services that support the organization's objectives.
evidence: Organizational mission and cybersecurity objectives documentation approved by leadership, showing how cybersecurity strategy aligns to business goals and stakeholder expectations
Legal, regulatory, and contractual obligation inventory mapping applicable requirements (e.g., HIPAA, GDPR, PCI DSS) to cybersecurity controls and ownership assignments
Stakeholder communication records, such as board-level cybersecurity briefings or risk committee meeting minutes, demonstrating executive understanding of cybersecurity context
Third-party and vendor dependency maps identifying critical service providers, their role in the organization's mission, and associated risk tier classifications

sub_controls.title: Organizational mission understood and used in cybersecurity risk management
Internal and external stakeholders identified and engaged
Legal and regulatory requirements understood
Critical objectives, capabilities, and services identified
Outcomes, capabilities, and services that the organization depends on are identified

controls.title: Risk Management Strategy
controls.description: The organization's priorities, constraints, risk tolerance, and assumptions are established and used to support operational risk decisions. A cybersecurity risk management strategy is defined and communicated to ensure consistent risk decision-making across the organization.
evidence: Cybersecurity risk management strategy document approved by senior leadership defining risk appetite, risk tolerance thresholds, and risk acceptance criteria
Risk register or risk management platform records showing identified cybersecurity risks, likelihood and impact ratings, treatment decisions, and residual risk tracking
Risk committee or governance body meeting minutes demonstrating periodic review of cybersecurity risk posture and alignment of risk decisions with organizational strategy
Risk treatment plan records including mitigation projects, risk acceptance approvals, and evidence of progress against planned risk reduction activities

sub_controls.title: Risk management objectives established and agreed to
Risk appetite and risk tolerance determined, expressed, and communicated
Organizational roles for risk management established
Strategic direction that describes appropriate risk response options identified
Lines of communication across the organization established for cybersecurity risks
A standardized method for calculating, documenting, categorizing, and prioritizing risks established
Strategic opportunities arising from cybersecurity risk management are characterized and used

controls.title: Roles, Responsibilities, and Authorities
controls.description: Cybersecurity roles, responsibilities, and authorities to foster accountability, performance assessment, and continuous improvement are established and communicated across the organization, ensuring that every individual understands their obligations for protecting information assets.
evidence: Cybersecurity roles and responsibilities matrix (RACI) defining ownership for key security functions, including policy ownership, incident response, and risk management
Job descriptions for cybersecurity and security-relevant roles confirming documented security responsibilities, with evidence of annual review and update
Organization chart and delegation of authority records showing the reporting structure for the CISO or equivalent role and escalation paths for cybersecurity decisions
Performance review records or security KPI dashboards demonstrating accountability mechanisms for personnel with cybersecurity responsibilities

sub_controls.title: Organizational leadership is responsible and accountable for cybersecurity risk and fosters a culture that is risk-aware
Roles, responsibilities, and authorities related to cybersecurity risk management are established, communicated, understood, and enforced
Adequate resources are allocated commensurate with the cybersecurity risk strategy
Cybersecurity is included in human resources practices

controls.title: Policy
controls.description: Organizational cybersecurity policy is established, communicated, and enforced. Policies reflect the organization's risk management strategy, address applicable legal and regulatory requirements, and are reviewed and updated on a defined cycle to remain current and effective.
evidence: Cybersecurity policy library with an index showing policy titles, owners, review dates, version history, and approval signatures from authorized leadership
Policy communication records demonstrating that all personnel have received and acknowledged cybersecurity policies, including new-hire onboarding attestation records
Policy exception management records showing a formal process for requesting, approving, documenting, and reviewing exceptions to cybersecurity policy requirements
Annual policy review evidence showing that policies have been assessed against changes in the threat landscape, regulatory environment, and organizational risk posture

sub_controls.title: Policy for managing cybersecurity risks is established based on organizational context and risk management objectives
Policy for managing cybersecurity risks is reviewed, updated, communicated, and enforced

controls.title: Oversight
controls.description: Results of organization-wide cybersecurity risk management activities and performance against defined objectives are used by leadership to inform, improve, and adjust the risk management strategy. Governance bodies provide oversight of the cybersecurity program's effectiveness.
evidence: Board or executive cybersecurity briefing materials and meeting minutes demonstrating leadership review of cybersecurity program performance, key metrics, and significant risks
Cybersecurity program performance dashboard or scorecard showing KPIs and KRIs tracked over time, with trend analysis and comparison against targets
Internal audit or third-party assessment reports evaluating the effectiveness of the cybersecurity program with management responses and remediation tracking
Annual cybersecurity program review documentation showing how lessons learned from incidents, exercises, and assessments have been incorporated into program improvements

sub_controls.title: Cybersecurity risk management strategy outcomes are reviewed to inform and adjust strategy
The cybersecurity risk management strategy is reviewed and adjusted to ensure coverage of organizational requirements
Organizational cybersecurity risk management performance is evaluated and reviewed for adjustments

controls.title: Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management
controls.description: Cyber supply chain risk management processes are identified, established, managed, monitored, and improved by organizational stakeholders to understand and adequately address risks associated with the products and services acquired from suppliers and other third parties.
evidence: Cybersecurity supply chain risk management (C-SCRM) policy and program documentation defining supplier risk assessment methodology, tiering criteria, and ongoing monitoring requirements
Supplier security assessment records including completed questionnaires, SOC 2 Type II reports, ISO 27001 certificates, or equivalent evidence reviewed during onboarding and periodically thereafter

Vendor contracts and agreements containing cybersecurity-specific clauses covering data protection obligations, security incident notification requirements, audit rights, and minimum security control expectations
Critical supplier inventory with risk tier classifications, recent assessment dates, and documented escalation or remediation actions for suppliers with identified deficiencies

sub_controls.title: A cybersecurity supply chain risk management program, strategy, objectives, policies, and processes are established
Cybersecurity roles and responsibilities for suppliers are established
Cybersecurity supply chain risk management is integrated into cybersecurity and enterprise risk management
Suppliers are known and prioritized by criticality
Requirements to address cybersecurity risks in supply chains are established, prioritized, and integrated into contracts
Planning and due diligence are performed to reduce risks before entering into formal supplier or other third-party relationships
Risks posed by a supplier, their products and services, and other third parties are identified, monitored, and responded to
Relevant suppliers and other third parties are included in incident planning
Supply chain security practices are integrated into cybersecurity and enterprise risk management programs
Cybersecurity supply chain risk management plans include provisions for activities that occur after the conclusion of a partnership or service agreement

controls.title: Asset Management
controls.description: Assets that enable the organization to achieve business purposes are identified and managed consistent with their relative importance to organizational objectives and the organization's risk strategy. Both hardware and software assets, as well as data assets and external dependencies, are inventoried and maintained.
evidence: Comprehensive enterprise asset inventory covering hardware, software, data, and external service assets, with ownership, classification, and criticality ratings assigned to each asset
Automated asset discovery tool reports (e.g., network scans, MDM enrollment reports) demonstrating continuous identification of assets connected to the enterprise environment
Data flow maps and data inventory documenting where sensitive data is stored, processed, and transmitted, including cross-border data flows and third-party sharing
Asset lifecycle management records including procurement, deployment, and disposal procedures, with confirmation that decommissioned assets are removed from inventories

sub_controls.title: Inventories of hardware managed by the organization are maintained
Inventories of software, services, and systems managed by the organization are maintained
Representations of the organization's authorized network communication and internal and external network data flows are maintained
Inventories of services provided by suppliers are maintained
Assets are prioritized based on classification, criticality, resources, and impact on the mission
Inventories of data and corresponding metadata for designated data types are maintained
Systems, hardware, software, services, and data are managed throughout their life cycles

controls.title: Risk Assessment
controls.description: The organization understands the cybersecurity risk to organizational operations, assets, and individuals. Threats, vulnerabilities, likelihoods, and impacts are identified and used to determine risk exposure, enabling informed decisions about risk treatment and prioritization.
evidence: Formal risk assessment methodology documentation describing the risk identification, analysis, and evaluation processes, including the likelihood and impact rating scales used
Risk assessment records for critical systems and processes showing identified threats, associated vulnerabilities, likelihood ratings, impact ratings, and resulting risk scores

Threat intelligence subscription or feed records demonstrating consumption of current threat actor tactics, techniques, and procedures (TTPs) relevant to the organization's industry and technology stack
Vulnerability scan reports integrated into the risk assessment process, showing how technical vulnerability findings are translated into risk register entries with treatment decisions

sub_controls.title: Vulnerabilities in assets are identified, validated, and recorded
Cyber threat intelligence is received from information sharing forums and sources
Internal and external threats to the organization are identified and recorded
Potential impacts and likelihoods of threats exploiting vulnerabilities are identified and recorded
Threats, vulnerabilities, likelihoods, and impacts are used to understand inherent risk and inform risk response prioritization
Risk responses are chosen, prioritized, planned, tracked, and communicated
Changes and exceptions are managed, assessed for risk impact, recorded, and tracked

controls.title: Improvement
controls.description: Improvements to organizational cybersecurity risk management processes, procedures, and activities are identified across all CSF Functions using lessons learned from cybersecurity activities, including incidents, assessments, exercises, and performance metrics.
evidence: Post-incident review and lessons-learned reports demonstrating that findings from security incidents are incorporated into updates to policies, procedures, and technical controls
Cybersecurity program improvement roadmap or corrective action plan showing prioritized initiatives identified from assessments, audits, and performance metric analysis
Tabletop exercise or penetration test after-action reports with documented gap remediation tracking and confirmed closure of identified improvement items
Metrics trend analysis reports showing improvement over time in key cybersecurity indicators such as mean time to detect, mean time to respond, and patch compliance rates

sub_controls.title: Improvements are identified from evaluations
Improvements are identified from security tests and exercises, including those done in coordination with suppliers and relevant third parties
Improvements are identified from execution of operational processes, procedures, and activities
Incident, vulnerability, and other cybersecurity findings are analyzed for root cause

controls.title: Exposure Management
controls.description: The organization identifies and manages cybersecurity exposure by continuously discovering, analyzing, and prioritizing vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, and exploitable weaknesses across the attack surface to reduce the window of opportunity for adversaries.
evidence: Continuous vulnerability scanning reports covering the full asset inventory, including authenticated scan results demonstrating detection of software vulnerabilities and misconfigurations
Attack surface management or exposure analysis records identifying externally reachable assets, open ports, and services with associated vulnerability findings and remediation status
Patch management compliance reports showing patching SLA adherence by severity tier, with exception records for systems that cannot be patched within standard windows
Vulnerability prioritization methodology documentation showing how CVSS scores, exploitability data (e.g., CISA KEV catalog), and business context are used to rank remediation efforts

controls.title: Identity Management and Access Control
controls.description: Access to physical and logical assets is limited to authorized users, services, and hardware, and is managed commensurate with the assessed risk of unauthorized access. The organization enforces strong authentication, applies least-privilege principles, and manages the full identity lifecycle.
evidence: Identity governance platform records showing provisioning workflows, access request approvals, access certification results, and timely revocation of access for terminated users
MFA enrollment and enforcement reports demonstrating that MFA is required for all users accessing organizational systems, with particular emphasis on remote access and privileged operations
Privileged access management (PAM) solution records including credential vaulting, session recording, just-in-time access provisioning, and privileged account audit logs
Access control policy and role matrix showing least-privilege role definitions, separation of duties enforcement, and periodic role review records with management attestation

sub_controls.title: Identities and credentials for authorized users, services, and hardware are managed by the organization
Identities are proofed and bound to credentials based on the context of interactions
Users, services, and hardware are authenticated
Identity assertions are protected, conveyed, and verified
Access permissions, entitlements, and authorizations are defined in a policy, managed, enforced, and reviewed
Physical access to assets is managed, monitored, and enforced commensurate with risk

controls.title: Awareness and Training
controls.description: The organization's personnel and partners are provided cybersecurity awareness and training so that they can perform their cybersecurity-related tasks. Training is role-appropriate, regularly updated, and effectiveness is measured to drive continuous improvement.
evidence: Security awareness training program documentation including annual curriculum review records, content update evidence reflecting current threats, and delivery platform configuration
Training completion reports for the review period showing all employees and relevant contractors completed required security awareness training within defined deadlines
Role-specific training completion records for privileged users, developers, and other high-risk roles covering topics such as secure coding, social engineering, and data handling
Phishing simulation program records including campaign configurations, click and report rates by department, and mandatory remediation training completion for users who failed simulations

sub_controls.title: Personnel are provided with awareness and training so that they possess the knowledge and skills to perform general tasks with cybersecurity risks in mind
Individuals in specialized roles are provided with awareness and training so that they possess the knowledge and skills to perform relevant tasks

controls.title: Data Security
controls.description: Data is managed consistent with the organization's risk strategy to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets. Controls address data at rest, data in transit, and data in use, and are commensurate with the sensitivity classification of the data.
evidence: Encryption-at-rest implementation evidence for databases, file systems, and cloud storage containing sensitive data, showing key management procedures and algorithm strength (e.g., AES-256)
Encryption-in-transit configuration evidence showing TLS 1.2 or higher enforced for all data transmission, with certificate management records and prohibited protocol scan results
DLP solution policy configuration and enforcement reports showing data handling rules for sensitive data categories, with alert and incident logs for the review period
Data classification policy implementation evidence including data tagging or labeling records, handling procedure compliance, and data masking configuration for non-production environments

sub_controls.title: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-at-rest are protected
The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-transit are protected
The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-use are protected
Backups of data are created, protected, maintained, and tested

controls.title: Platform Security
controls.description: The hardware, software, and services of physical and virtual platforms are managed consistent with the organization's risk strategy to protect their confidentiality, integrity, and availability. Platforms are configured securely, hardened against known attack techniques, and maintained through patching and lifecycle management.
evidence: Platform hardening standards for each technology type (servers, workstations, network devices, cloud instances, containers) with evidence of review against current CIS Benchmarks or equivalent
Configuration compliance scan results showing adherence to hardening baselines across the platform inventory, with remediation tracking for identified deviations
Patch management reports demonstrating timely application of security patches across all platform types, with SLA compliance metrics and exception documentation
Configuration management database (CMDB) records showing approved baseline versions for all platforms, change history, and drift detection alert response records

sub_controls.title: Configuration management practices are established and applied
Software is maintained, replaced, and removed commensurate with risk
Hardware is maintained, replaced, and removed commensurate with risk
Log records are generated and made available for continuous monitoring
Installation and execution of unauthorized software are prevented
Secure software development practices are integrated, and their security is evaluated

controls.title: Technology Infrastructure Resilience
controls.description: Security architectures are managed with the organization's risk strategy to protect asset and organizational resilience. Resilience capabilities including backup, redundancy, and recovery are implemented and tested to ensure continued availability of critical systems and data.
evidence: Backup policy and implementation records defining backup scope, frequency, retention, and off-site or cloud storage requirements, with job completion logs for the review period
Disaster recovery plan (DRP) documentation defining recovery time objectives (RTOs) and recovery point objectives (RPOs) for critical systems, with evidence of annual review
Business continuity and disaster recovery test records showing successful restoration of critical systems within defined RTO/RPO targets, with gap remediation actions from failed tests
High availability and redundancy architecture documentation for critical systems, including evidence of tested failover procedures and confirmation that single points of failure have been addressed

sub_controls.title: Networks and environments are protected from unauthorized logical access and usage
The organization's technology assets are protected from environmental threats
Mechanisms are implemented to achieve resilience requirements in normal and adverse situations
Adequate resource capacity to ensure availability is maintained

controls.title: Network Access Management
controls.description: Communications and data flows are protected by managing network access at the perimeter and within the network through segmentation, filtering, and monitoring controls to prevent unauthorized access and limit the lateral movement of adversaries.
evidence: Network segmentation architecture documentation showing defined trust zones, inter-zone firewall policies, and evidence that sensitive environments (e.g., production, PCI, OT) are isolated from general networks
Firewall policy review records demonstrating periodic rule set review, removal of stale rules, and management sign-off confirming continued business necessity for each permitted rule
VPN or remote access configuration evidence showing encrypted tunnels, MFA enforcement, and split-tunneling policy aligned to the organization's security requirements
Zero-trust architecture documentation or network access control (NAC) records showing device compliance verification, user identity validation, and least-privilege network access enforcement

controls.title: Adverse Event Analysis
controls.description: Anomalies, indicators of compromise, and other potentially adverse events are analyzed to characterize the events and detect cybersecurity incidents. Collected log data and threat intelligence are correlated to identify patterns indicative of malicious activity and reduce false positives.
evidence: SIEM correlation rule library documenting detection logic for priority attack patterns (e.g., credential stuffing, lateral movement, data exfiltration), with evidence of tuning and effectiveness review
Log aggregation coverage report showing that critical event sources (authentication systems, endpoints, network devices, cloud services) are onboarded and actively forwarding to the SIEM
Security alert triage records demonstrating analyst investigation of SIEM-generated alerts, with documented disposition (true positive, false positive), escalation decisions, and response actions
Threat intelligence integration records showing how indicators of compromise (IOCs) are ingested into detection tooling and correlated against log data to identify potential incidents

sub_controls.title: Potentially adverse events are analyzed to better understand associated activities
Information is correlated from multiple sources
The estimated impact and scope of adverse events are understood
Information on adverse events is provided to authorized staff and tools
Cyber threat intelligence and other contextual information are integrated into the analysis
Incidents are declared when adverse events meet the defined incident criteria

controls.title: Continuous Monitoring
controls.description: Assets are monitored to find anomalies, indicators of compromise, and other potentially adverse events. Monitoring capabilities span network, endpoint, user behavior, and cloud environments to provide broad visibility and enable timely detection of security-relevant events.
evidence: Security monitoring program documentation defining monitored assets, event sources, monitoring frequency, alert thresholds, and analyst response procedures
Network monitoring tool deployment evidence (e.g., IDS/IPS, NDR, NetFlow analysis) showing sensor coverage at critical network segments with recent detection statistics
EDR platform deployment report showing agent coverage across endpoints, active detection policies, and behavioral alert review records for the assessment period
Cloud security posture management (CSPM) tool reports showing continuous assessment of cloud environment configurations against security benchmarks, with finding remediation records

sub_controls.title: Networks and network services are monitored to find potentially adverse events
The physical environment is monitored to find potentially adverse events
Personnel activity and technology usage are monitored to find potentially adverse events
External service provider activities and services are monitored to find potentially adverse events
Computing hardware and software, runtime environments, and their data are monitored to find potentially adverse events

controls.title: Incident Management
controls.description: Incidents are identified and analyzed through a defined process that establishes detection thresholds, categorizes events, and escalates confirmed incidents to the appropriate response teams. Detection capabilities are validated and refined based on lessons learned.
evidence: Incident classification and escalation matrix defining event severity tiers, criteria for declaring an incident, and notification requirements by severity level
Incident detection and triage records showing the flow from initial alert to confirmed incident declaration, including analyst investigation notes and escalation timestamps
Detection effectiveness metrics tracking the rate of confirmed true positives versus false positives, mean time to detect, and analyst workload distribution across the review period
SOAR playbook documentation and execution logs showing automated enrichment, triage, and escalation actions that support efficient incident identification and classification

controls.title: Incident Management
controls.description: Responses to detected cybersecurity incidents are managed according to a defined incident response plan. Response activities are coordinated to contain the incident, minimize impact, and restore normal operations as quickly as possible, with all actions documented for accountability and post-incident review.
evidence: Incident response plan (IRP) documentation including response playbooks for priority incident types (e.g., ransomware, data breach, insider threat), with evidence of annual review and update
Incident response records for the review period showing documented containment actions, affected system isolation evidence, and timeline of response activities with owner accountability
Incident management platform or ticketing system records demonstrating structured tracking of incident lifecycle from detection through closure, including SLA compliance metrics
SOAR platform orchestration records showing automated containment playbook execution, analyst decision prompts, and audit trail of automated actions taken during incident response

sub_controls.title: The incident response plan is executed in coordination with relevant third parties once an incident is declared
Incident reports are triaged and validated
Incidents are categorized and prioritized
Incidents are escalated or elevated as needed
The criteria for initiating incident recovery are applied

controls.title: Incident Analysis
controls.description: Investigations are conducted to ensure effective response and support forensic analysis. The organization establishes and preserves evidence, conducts root cause analysis, and determines the full scope and impact of incidents to inform containment, eradication, and future prevention.
evidence: Digital forensics investigation records for significant incidents including evidence collection procedures, chain of custody documentation, and forensic analysis findings
Log preservation and legal hold records demonstrating that forensically relevant logs were secured and protected from modification upon incident declaration
Root cause analysis (RCA) reports for significant incidents identifying the initial access vector, attack progression, contributing factors, and recommended corrective actions
SIEM query and investigation records showing analyst use of log correlation to reconstruct the attack timeline, identify affected assets, and determine the scope of compromise

sub_controls.title: Analysis is performed to establish what has taken place during an incident and the root cause of the incident
Actions performed during an investigation are recorded
Incident cause is determined
Incidents are categorized consistent with response plans

controls.title: Incident Response Reporting and Communication
controls.description: Response activities are coordinated with internal and external stakeholders as required by applicable laws, regulations, and policies. Communication plans ensure timely notification of affected parties, regulatory bodies, law enforcement, and the public as required by the nature and scope of the incident.
evidence: Incident communication plan defining notification thresholds, internal escalation paths, external notification requirements, and templates for stakeholder and regulatory communications

Breach notification records for incidents involving personal or regulated data, showing notifications delivered within required regulatory timeframes (e.g., 72 hours under GDPR, state breach notification laws)
Regulatory or law enforcement notification records where required, including documentation of communications and any guidance received from authorities during the incident
Internal incident status update records showing regular communication to executive leadership and relevant business stakeholders during active incident response

sub_controls.title: Internal and external stakeholders are notified of incidents
Information is shared with designated internal and external stakeholders as well as authorized authorities

controls.title: Incident Mitigation
controls.description: Activities are performed to prevent expansion of an event, mitigate its effects, and eradicate the incident. Mitigation efforts include isolating affected systems, removing malicious artifacts, patching exploited vulnerabilities, and validating that all threat actor access has been eliminated.
evidence: Incident eradication records documenting the removal of malware, unauthorized access paths, and persistence mechanisms, with validation scans confirming successful remediation
Endpoint isolation and containment evidence showing affected systems were quarantined promptly after incident confirmation, with EDR-initiated containment action logs
Vulnerability remediation records for the exploited weakness that enabled the incident, including patching evidence or compensating control implementation with validation confirmation
Post-eradication validation evidence such as clean scan results, credential rotation records, and confirmation that affected systems were rebuilt or restored from known-good backups

sub_controls.title: Incidents are contained
Incidents are eradicated

controls.title: Incident Recovery Plan Execution
controls.description: Restoration activities are performed to ensure operational availability of systems and services affected by cybersecurity incidents. Recovery is executed according to the approved recovery plan, with priority given to critical business functions, and recovery actions are documented for after-action review.
evidence: Disaster recovery plan execution records for incidents requiring system restoration, including documented recovery steps taken, timestamps, personnel involved, and sign-off on recovery completion
System restoration evidence from known-good backups, including backup integrity verification records and confirmation that restored systems were scanned clean before returning to production
Recovery time tracking records showing actual recovery durations compared to defined RTOs, with root cause analysis for any instances where RTOs were not met
Business continuity activation records where applicable, including invocation decision authority, alternate site or cloud failover evidence, and communication to affected business units

sub_controls.title: The recovery portion of the incident response plan is executed once initiated from the incident response process
Recovery actions are selected, scoped, prioritized, and performed
The integrity of backups and other restoration assets is verified before using them for restoration
Critical mission functions and cybersecurity risk management are considered to establish post-incident operational norms
The integrity of restored assets is verified, systems and services are restored, and normal operating status is confirmed
The end of incident recovery is declared based on criteria, and incident-related documentation is completed

controls.title: Incident Recovery Communication
controls.description: Restoration activities are coordinated with internal and external parties. Communications to internal stakeholders, customers, partners, and the public accurately represent the recovery status and are issued in a manner that supports trust and transparency while protecting sensitive investigation details.
evidence: Recovery communication plan defining the process for issuing status updates to internal stakeholders, customers, and partners during the recovery phase of a cybersecurity incident
Stakeholder communication records issued during the recovery phase, including internal status updates, customer notifications, and public statements with review and approval records
Post-incident communication review records assessing whether communications were accurate, timely, and aligned with the organization's crisis communication policy
After-action report documenting the full incident timeline, business impact, recovery actions, lessons learned, and improvement commitments shared with relevant leadership

sub_controls.title: Recovery activities and progress in restoring operational capabilities are communicated to designated internal and external stakeholders
Public updates on incident recovery are shared using approved methods and messaging
